items.title: 'It hurts' - Saints boss Eckert on Spygate judgement
items.summary: Southampton head coach Tonda Eckert admits it has been "tough to read" judgement on his role in the scandal that saw the club kicked out of the Championship play-offs

items.title: 'No regrets' - Tuchel on England's defeat by Argentina
items.summary: A number of England players - including more than one senior player - were bemused by Thomas Tuchel's defensive substitutions in the World Cup semi-final against Argentina, Sky Sports News understands

items.title: Jarrod Bowen declares intention to stay at West Ham despite relegation
items.summary: Captain says promotion would bring ‘the most happiness’ He visited largest shareholder Daniel Kretinsky in Prague Jarrod Bowen has said he intends to remain with West Ham after the club’s relegation to the Championship. The forward has attracted interest from Premier League teams but has spelled out his desire to stay after a summer during which he travelled to Prague for talks with the largest shareholder, Daniel Kretinsky. “I feel like we’re moving in the right direction as a club,” Bowen told West Ham ’s media channels.

items.title: Villa sign Newcastle target Manzambi in club-record deal
items.summary: Aston Villa have beaten Newcastle to the signing of Johan Manzambi after completing a club-record £59.5m deal for the midfielder from Freiburg

items.title: Tuchel urges England to finish strongly in emotional address
items.summary: England manager Thomas Tuchel urges his players to end the World Cup strongly in an emotional address after their agonising semi-final defeat by Argentina

items.title: Stick with Tuchel unless Guardiola is available - Rooney
items.summary: Wayne Rooney thinks the Football Association should stick with Thomas Tuchel as England head coach unless a move for Pep Guardiola is possible

items.title: Ederson extends Atalanta contract after Man Utd move collapses
items.summary: Atalanta midfielder Ederson extends his contract after a move to Manchester United collapses

items.title: Who could follow as Gordon leaves Scotland as a legend?
items.summary: Craig Gordon has retired as a goalkeeping legend with Heart of Midlothian and Scotland, but what is his legacy and who can succeed him with the national team?

items.title: Iheanacho rejects new Celtic deal for Turkey's second tier
items.summary: Nigeria striker Kelechi Iheanacho turns down a new contract with Celtic and joins Bursaspor, who have just been promoted to Turkey's second tier

items.title: Leeds sign £34.1m defender Muharemovic
items.summary: Leeds United sign defender Tarik Muharemovic for £34.1m from Serie A side Sassuolo

items.title: Szoboszlai signs new long-term Liverpool deal
items.summary: Dominik Szoboszlai has signed a new deal at Liverpool; The 25-year-olds new contract keeps him at Anfield until 2031; Szoboszlai moved to Liverpool for £60m from RB Leipzig in the summer of 2023

items.title: Why Arsenal have made Rogers their top forward target this summer
items.summary: It takes something special to earn the trust of Unai Emery, force your way into Thomas Tuchel's England plans and attract the interest of Europe's elite. Morgan Rogers has done all three and that is why Arsenal are looking to make him their latest statement signing

items.title: Dominik Szoboszlai signs Liverpool deal as Aston Villa go for João Gomes
items.summary: Contract to 2031 one of his ‘top three’ life moments Leeds close to signing centre-back Muharemovic Dominik Szoboszlai has given Liverpool and their head coach, Andoni Iraola , a significant boost by signing a new contract to 2031. The midfielder, who was the team’s best performer during a difficult final season under Arne Slot, has two years on his deal and mentioned several times last season that there had been no real progress over an extension. After months of negotiations between Liverpool’s sporting director, Richard Hughes, and Szoboszlai’s representatives, the 25-year-old has signed the deal.

items.title: More than 200 countries endorse Infantino for fourth Fifa term despite Balogun scandal
items.summary: Only a handful of FAs have not declared their support Uefa has made its opposition clear on number of issues Gianni Infantino has the formal endorsement of more than 200 countries for re-election as Fifa’s president despite the climate of unrest that has swirled since the scandal surrounding Folarin Balogun’s reprieve from suspension. The Guardian understands only a handful of Fifa’s 211 member associations are still to send letters of support for Infantino, who is on course to be voted into a fourth term by a landslide at its congress in March. A small number of European countries are among the outliers, with Germany the highest-profile FA yet to provide official backing.

items.title: Arsenal close in on £34m deal for Club Brugge forward Christos Tzolis
items.summary: Greece international replacement for Leandro Trossard Arsenal seek defensive cover after William Saliba injury Arsenal have agreed a deal worth up to €40m (£34m) with Club Brugge to sign Christos Tzolis as a replacement for Leandro Trossard and will step up their attempts to sign defensive cover due to concerns that William Saliba could miss the start of the new season due to his ongoing back issues. An official bid for Tzolis, a Greece international who scored 22 goals last season and was previously targeted by Crystal Palace, has yet to be submitted but it is understood that Arsenal have verbally agreed to pay the Belgian club’s asking price. The 24-year-old is expected to have his medical in the coming days and sign a long-term contract.

items.title: How are London City Lionesses able to embark on such a transfer spree?
items.summary: Many are in awe of the investment that Michele Kang is making into the women’s game. Others, though, are baffled There is one team whose transfer activity this summer has captured everyone’s attention. London City Lionesses, a club who finished in the bottom half of the second tier a little over two years ago, have stunned the women’s game by signing a flurry of big names, including the Spain stars Alexia Putellas and Mapi León, the former England goalkeeper Mary Earps, and then, on Wednesday morning, the France winger Kadidiatou Diani.

items.title: Didier Deschamps pays price for breaking free from the shackles that led him to glory | Jonathan Wilson
items.summary: France finally let loose all their attacking power in this tournament and yet when the big test came against Spain they were too open Maybe Didier Deschamps was right all along. He has been criticised over his 14 years in the France job for being too cautious, for prioritising control, for not unleashing his great fleet of attacking players. In this tournament, his last as national manager, Deschamps has relaxed – from a tactical point of view at least; he remains as grouchy as ever in his public utterances.

items.title: ‘I need to prove I belong here’: Andoni Iraola stays humble in new Liverpool era
items.summary: Head coach has no illusions as to the scale of the task and wants to start by reconnecting the team and supporters It was Andoni Iraola’s humility that made the biggest impression at his unveiling as Liverpool’s new head coach on Monday. Without fanfare or bombast the 44-year-old promised not to change as a result of landing the biggest job of his coaching career and also to connect with club and city alike. “The magic of being the Liverpool manager,” as he nicely described the opportunity.

items.title: Julián Álvarez’s extra-time stunner sinks 10-man Switzerland to send Argentina into semi-finals
items.summary: Did anyone think they would do it the easy way? If Argentina are to win this World Cup they will only get there via rollercoaster. A seemingly straightforward night’s work against Switzerland became their tournament in microcosm, threatening to squander it all before finding salvation through a moment of unfettered genius.

items.title: Krönika: Mäster Skräddare
items.summary: Denna artikel är byggd kring den klassiska historien ”Mäster Skräddare”, där en man går till skräddaren för att få en rock sydd. Det blir dock ingen rock, utan för varje vecka han kommer dit så blir plagget mindre och mindre, tills det inte blir något alls. Den artikel skall läsas med STOR ironi, då den inte är menad att skada någon på något sätt utan bara få er läsare att tänka till och kanske, eventuellt, få ett gott skratt

items.title: Kafaji klar för London City – Fotbollssurr om övergången
items.summary: Rosa Kafaji är klar för London City Lionesses. Den 23-åriga svenska landslagsspelaren lämnar Arsenal och har skrivit på ett treårskontrakt med den ambitiösa WSL-klubben. Här är vad Linda Fotbollssurr tycker om övergången

items.title: Sam Larsson återvänder till IFK Göteborg: ”En dundervärvning”
items.summary: IFK Göteborg har presenterat ett tungt sommarförvärv. Sam Larsson är tillbaka i Blåvitt efter tolv år utomlands och ansluter omedelbart på fri transfer. Arvid Larsson från SvenskaFans IFK-redaktion har länge hoppats på en återkomst

items.title: Röster efter matchen: "En härlig energi"
items.summary: Det var idel leenden och stor lättnad i den himmelsblå zonen efter 4–0-krossen mot IFK Göteborg. Himmelriket tog tempen på tränare Gaute Hellstrup, debutmålskytten Anton Höög, en iskall Malte Frejd Pålsson och tvåmålsskytten Erik Botheim

items.title: 4 snabba efter Hammarbys seger mot Kalmar
items.summary: Hammarby tog en säker seger med 2-0 mot Kalmar hemma på Nya Söderstadion. Målskyttarna var Paulos Abraham och självmål (efter ett hårt inspel från höger från Montader Madjed). Henrik Rydström tog därmed sin andra raka seger sedan han tog över som ny tränare för Hammarby

items.title: Rydström: ”Hur lojala ska tränare vara?”
items.summary: I sin första hemmamatch som tränare för Hammarby IF ställs Henrik Rydström mot sitt gamla lag Kalmar FF. SvenskaFans träffade Rydström i veckan för ett samtal om historiken med Kalmar, Nanne Bergstrands betydelse för honom och tränarlojalitet

items.title: Panel inför matchen mot Kalmar
items.summary: Vi på S@derstadion tar till ett nygammalt grepp och ersätter den traditionella inför-artikeln med en inför-panel. Det handlar förstås om söndagens match mot Kalmar, där nya tränaren Henrik Rydström får möta det lag som han både spelade i under många år, för att sedan även bli tränare där. De som medverkar i panelen denna gång är Linus Isaksson, Elin Ehrencrona och Oliver Jernelius

items.title: Bryson DeChambeau straffas efter fusk i The Open
items.summary: Bryson DeChambeau fuskade under The Open. – Bryson har fått två slags plikt, säger chefsdomare Grant Moir

items.title: Sam Larsson om återkomsten: ”Inte nu du ska fråga om det”
items.summary: Sam Larsson var tillbaka i IFK Göteborg efter tolv år. Han hoppade in och fick en assist vid segermålet 2–1 mot BP. – Det var magiskt att få med oss tre poäng, säger Larsson till SVT Sport

items.title: Viktor Gustafsons ilska över Mamadou Diagnes satsning
items.summary: Viktor Gustafson var minst sagt irriterad över en situation alla verkade ha missat när Mjällby tog emot Västerås. Mittfältaren förklarar att Mamadou Diagne stämplade honom över hälen. – Är inte det rött kort så kan man ju gå runt och slå varandra på planen, säger Gustafson

items.title: Mållöst mellan Mjällby och Västerås – i Elliot Strouds avsked (?)
items.summary: Någon sprakande målfest bjöds det inte på i Hällevik på fredagskvällen. Matchen mellan Mjällby och Västerås slutade mållöst – i vad som kan ha varit Elliot Strouds avsked. Men frågan är om inte VSK borde avgått med segern.

items.title: Rött kort bakom Göteborgs första hemmaseger för säsongen
items.summary: Krisande IFK Göteborg tog första hemmasegern för säsongen när BP besegrades med 2–1. Ett rött kort gav en man mer i en timme, vilket grundlade segern i den dramatiska matchen

items.title: Ludvig Åberg klättrar efter tuffa starten i The Open
items.summary: Efter en tuff första dag bjöd den svenske stjärnan på inte mindre än sju birdies under den andra rundan av The Open, och gick runt på 66 slag. Han är nu tre under par inför fortsättningen. – Jag ska försöka jaga ikapp ledarna, säger han till Viaplay

items.title: Jesper Svensson nära HIO i The Open – stod för fin runda
items.summary: En första runda han helst ville glömma. Men på dag två av The Open steppade Jesper Svensson upp. På det femte hålet var han nära en hole-in-one – men fick nöja sig med eagle.

items.title: Doldisen Lucas Herbert efter superrundan i The Open: ”I rekordböckerna”
items.summary: Doldisen Lucas Herbert gick runt den andra rundan i The Open på 62 slag och noterades för dubbla tangerade rekord. Tangerat banrekord och den tangerat lägsta scoren någonsin under en runda på en major

items.title: World Cup of memes: Japan fans, Beckham unbothered and a simmering bromance
items.summary: A tournament with more teams and more games has been filled with euphoria, heartbreak, and these weird and wonderful viral moments As a Japanese supporter exclaimed to a television camera, “Texas is good, everything is big”, we knew the World Cup had started. In those early euphoric moments, seeing the palpable joy when everything is still possible, it seemed Japan fans were the MVPs of memes. They came in their thousands, dressed in sombreros and Mario outfits, tossed into the air like confetti and line dancing with the Dutch.

items.title: Genuine hope may have been fleeting for England. But it was still life-affirming | Max Rushden
items.summary: That’s how long I really had hope of reaching the World Cup final. And it didn’t kill me In her book Hope in the Dark the author Rebecca Solnit examines if it is possible to have hope when you consider all of human suffering. She quotes the Bulgarian writer Maria Popova: “Critical thinking without hope is cynicism, but hope without critical thinking is naivety.” By all accounts it is a compelling argument for hope as a catalyst for social change.

items.title: BBC v ITV: who won the UK’s battle of the World Cup 2026 broadcasters?
items.summary: Danny Murphy’s decision to start talking about his deceased cat will haunt the rest of his career while Christina Unkel’s referee analysis was no-nonsense and to the point The final may be Argentina v Spain, but ardent media watchers in the UK know that in broadcasting terms, a World Cup is always a domestic battle between the BBC and ITV. So who won on these important criteria?

items.title: Mjällby AIF – Västerås SK: Han hade förtjänat ett bättre avslut
items.summary: Förra säsongen var på många sätt ett mirakel. Vi hade marginalerna med oss, men framför allt spelade vi en fantastisk fotboll. När Røjkjær lämnade inför den här säsongen var det många som befarade det värsta.

items.title: Blåvitt-redaktören: ”Vi måste belysa problemet”
items.summary: IFK Göteborg tog en efterlängtad seger mot Brommapojkarna. Men trots en man mer satt det långt inne. Redaktör Arvid Larsson gläds åt segern men kan inte blunda för Blåvitts problem

items.title: Falkenberg vann träningsmatchen mot Laholms FK
items.summary: Under fredagen spelade FFF sin andra träningsmatch efter uppehållet. Nu var det Laholm som stod för motståndet och det blev en minst sagt rolig kväll när de gulvita vann med hela 6-0
